summary:
Shape analysis has no established role in differentiating free-floating intracarotid thrombuses from atheromatous plaques using CTA; existing evidence focuses on gross features such as filling defects and vascular narrowing without supporting quantifiable shape metrics. Clinical judgment remains primary due to lack of validation for algorithm-driven shape assessment in these contexts.

The summary above reflects what was derived directly from the given information—without adding external knowledge beyond the scope presented.
